title: Shinjis Role in Seeles Instrumentality split
display_title: Shinjis Role in Seeles Instrumentality split
wikitext:
[]: Shinji's Role in Seele's Instrumentality [split]             Quote Post by ThanatosII » Tue Sep 16, 2014 5:56 pm [Split from "Was Rei really wrong?" - Monk.] NemZ wrote: I think the gun analogy is flawed because 3rd impact was already in progress with him in the center of it all before Rei even got involved. In effect he'd already been handed a loaded gun and she just did a less then stellar attempt to talk him out of using it poorly, but still a damn sight better than letting the harpies/Seele run the show.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Sep 16, 2014 6:12 pm I'm saying that just with 01, the harpies and the lance that instrumentality was going to happen anyway, and per Fuyutsuki's comment we can infer Shinji was going to be critically involved somehow. Rei hijacked the process though, so we'll never know for sure what would have happened otherwise.

[]:             Quote Post by ThanatosII » Tue Sep 16, 2014 6:28 pm NemZ wrote: I'm saying that just with 01, the harpies and the lance that instrumentality was going to happen anyway, and per Fuyutsuki's comment we can infer Shinji was going to be critically involved somehow. Rei hijacked the process though, so we'll never know for sure what would have happened otherwise.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Sep 16, 2014 6:38 pm NemZ wrote: I don't agree with the idea that she was somehow forced into this as a mere puppet of alien programing and ritual. She was compelled to act because she cares about Shinji and is in a position to do something about it, that's all.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Sep 16, 2014 7:09 pm Even with the mind meld there's limits to how much one person can handle. I doubt Shinji's mind could deal with the fullness of Lilith. She'd snuff out an individual soul like a candle in a wind.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Sep 16, 2014 7:29 pm Hmm. Watching the relevant scene again just now it still isn't clear just what the hell the actual plan is. I think at first they were planning on just going with the MPEs and 01 somehow, but when the spear came back they reverted to the original plan to use Lilith somehow, though it certainly appears that she took control of the proceedings directly after her arrival to the sky party. Regardless, Shinji was going to be right in the middle of it no matter what happened. Bagheera wrote: But in that case she's very much responsible for her own actions, and thus very much in the wrong.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Sep 16, 2014 7:42 pm NemZ wrote: Hmm. Watching the relevant scene again just now it still isn't clear just what the hell the actual plan is. I think at first they were planning on just going with the MPEs and 01 somehow, but when the spear came back they reverted to the original plan to use Lilith somehow, though it certainly appears that she took control of the proceedings directly after her arrival to the sky party.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Sep 16, 2014 8:06 pm Bagheera wrote: I think that, once the Lance showed up,

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Sep 16, 2014 8:48 pm NemZ wrote: and per Fuyutsuki's comment we can infer Shinji was going to be critically involved somehow.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Sep 16, 2014 8:50 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: Seele also talks about Shinji as if he had been involved in their plan from the beginning, with all their "Through the destruction of the pilot's ego" stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Sep 16, 2014 8:52 pm да. If we thought Gendo was a schemer, Seele was even worse.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Sep 16, 2014 9:09 pm Bagheera wrote: Which makes the Kaworu plot really fucking grim .

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Sep 16, 2014 9:47 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: Seele also talks about Shinji as if he had been involved in their plan from the beginning, with all their "Through the destruction of the pilot's ego" stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Sep 16, 2014 9:48 pm ...hrm. Without Shinji, there's no 3I. That doesn't seem very smart of Seele, now that I think of it.

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Tue Sep 16, 2014 9:58 pm Monk Ed wrote: The fact that they ordered him killed indicates he was very much not part of the original plan. What they said is more likely along the lines of "You there at the switch with the power to either make things go the way we want or fuck everything up, make things go the way we want!".

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Sep 16, 2014 9:59 pm Asuka was meant to be the sacrifice all along.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Sep 16, 2014 10:00 pm Would it be possible for you to explain yourself without using religious theory?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Sep 16, 2014 10:02 pm Religion does not enter into my theories at all. Magic is not religion. Look at the life she led and the way she was treated. She was groomed her entire life for a psychological break.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Sep 16, 2014 10:04 pm Do you mean that Anno created her character for the sole purpose of serving as a sacrifice, or that forces in-universe specifically groomed her for it? I have a hard time believing either one of them off the bat, so I'd love to hear a more in-depth explanation so that I can understand you.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Sep 16, 2014 10:10 pm xanderkh wrote: In order for any great plan to come to fruition, you must prepare for EVERY POSSIBLE contingency. That means, every aspect is and must be easily expendable and replaceable, should the need arise.
